data.name: ActionChanging_helpMark
data.value:
Controls whether Actions on your Hotbar will be Replaced with combos from the plugin. If disabled, your manual presses of abilities will no longer be affected by any QoL Tweaks settings.
Auto-Rotation will work regardless of the setting.

data.name: ActionChanging_recommendedValue
data.value: On (This is essentially turning OFF most of QoL Tweaks)

data.name: ActionChanging_warningMark
data.value: QoL Tweaks is largely designed with Action Replacing in mind. Only Auto-Rotation will work if this is disabled. Disabling it may also lead to unexpected behavior, such as regarding Retargeting.

data.name: AddOutOfPartyNPCsToRetargeting_helpMark
data.value:
This will add any NPCs that are not in your party to the retargeting logic for healing actions.
Useful for healers who want to be able to target NPCs that are not in their party, such as quest NPCs. These NPCs will not generally work with any role-based custom Heal Stack entries (even if an NPC looks like a tank, they're not always classified as one)

data.name: AddOutOfPartyNPCsToRetargeting_recommendedValue
data.value: On (If you use Retargeting at all)

data.name: BlockSpellOnMove_helpMark
data.value: Will completely block actions while moving, by replacing Combo outputs with Savage Blade. This would supersede combo-specific movement options, which many jobs have.

data.name: BlockSpellOnMove_recommendedValue
data.value: Off (Most Jobs will handle this better with their Features)

data.name: CustomHealStack_helpMark
data.value:
If there are fewer than 4 items, and all return nothing when checked, will fall back to Self.
These targets will only be considered valid if they are friendly and within 25y.
When applied to something like Esuna, these targets will be checked for having a Cleansable Debuff, etc. So adding 'Any Cleansable Ally' or similar is not necessary.

data.name: EnabledOutputLog_helpMark
data.value: Will print to chat every time you use an action provided by QoL Tweaks.

data.name: EnabledOutputLog_recommendedValue
data.value: On (IF trying to report an issue)

data.name: HideChildren_helpMark
data.value: Will hide the Features and Options under disabled Combos.

data.name: HideConflictedCombos_helpMark
data.value: Will hide Combos that conflict with Combos that you have enabled.

data.name: HideMessageOfTheDay_helpMark
data.value: Will prevent the Message of the Day from being shown in your chat upon login.

data.name: InterruptDelay_helpMark
data.value: Controls the percentage of a total cast time to wait before interrupting enemy casts. Applies to all interrupts (including stuns used to interrupt) in every Job's Combos.

data.name: InterruptDelay_recommendedValue
data.value: below 40 (Above that and you start failing to interrupt many short casts)

data.name: MaximumWeavesPerWindow_helpMark
data.value: Controls how many oGCDs are allowed between GCDs. The 'default' for the game is double weaving, but triple weaving is completely possible with low enough latency (of every kind);but if you struggle with latency of any sort then single weaving may even be a good answer to try for you. Triple weaving is already done in a manner where we try to avoid clipping GCDs, and as such doesn't happen particularly often even if you have good latency, and is a valid thing to do, so it is a safe option if you want.

data.name: MeleeOffset_helpMark
data.value: Controls what is considered to be in melee range. Mainly for those who don't want to switch to ranged attacks if the boss walks slightly outside of range. For example a value of -0.5 would make you have to be 0.5 yalms closer to the target, or a value of 2 would allow you to be 2 yalms further away and still be considered in melee range (melee actions wouldn't work, but it would give you some warning instead of just suddenly doing less optimal actions).

data.name: MovementLeeway_helpMark
data.value: This controls how long of a delay is needed before QoL Tweaks recognizes you as moving. This allows you to not have to worry about small movements affecting your rotation, primarily for casters.

data.name: MovementLeeway_recommendedValue
data.value: 0.0-1.0 (Above that gets into the territory of breaking any Movement Options in your Job)

data.name: OpenerTimeout_helpMark
data.value: Controls how long of a gap with no action is allowed in an Opener, before it is considered failed and normal rotation is resumed. Can be necessary for some casters to increase, particularly when the first action of an Opener is a hard-cast.

data.name: OpenToCurrentJobOnSwitch_helpMark
data.value: Will automatically switch the PvE Features tab to the job you are currently playing, when you switch jobs.

data.name: OpenToCurrentJobOnSwitch_Name
data.value: Open PvE Features Tab to Current Job on Switching Jobs

data.name: OpenToCurrentJob_helpMark
data.value: When the PvE Features tab is opened it will automatically open to your current Job.

data.name: OpenToCurrentJob_Name
data.value: Open PvE Features Tab to Current Job on Opening

data.name: OpenToPvE_helpMark
data.value: When you open QoL Tweaks with `/qoltweaks`, it will open to the PvE Features tab, instead of the last tab you were on. Same as always using the `/qoltweaks pve` command to open QoL Tweaks.

data.name: OpenToPvE_Name
data.value: Open QoL Tweaks to the PvE Features Tab

data.name: OpenToPvP_helpMark
data.value: Same as above, when you open QoL Tweaks with `/qoltweaks`, it will open to the PvP Features tab, instead of the last tab you were on, when in a PvP area. Similar to using the `/qoltweaks pvp` command to open QoL Tweaks.

data.name: OpenToPvP_Name
data.value: Open QoL Tweaks to the PvP Features Tab in PvP areas

data.name: OutputOpenerLogs_helpMark
data.value: Will print the status of your Job's Opener to chat. e.g. When it is Ready, Fails, or Finishes.

data.name: OutputOpenerLogs_recommendedValue
data.value: On (IF trying to troubleshoot an Opener)

data.name: OverwriteQueue_helpMark
data.value: This will allow you to overwrite whatever is currently queued up with another action.

data.name: QueueAdjustThreshold_helpMark
data.value: Will allow you to queue when the GCD is at this time or less.

data.name: QueueAdjustThreshold_warningMark
data.value: Setting this too low or to zero will make it really hard to manually queue.

data.name: QueueAdjust_helpMark
data.value: Allows you to adjust your queue window to any time during the GCD rather than just within the last 0.3-0.5s. Useful if you're not mashing the key or using Auto-Rotation and wish to manually intervene.

data.name: RaiseStack_defaultValue
data.value: Any Healer > Any Tank > Any Raiser > Any Dead Party Member

data.name: RaiseStack_helpMark
data.value:
This is the order in which QoL Tweaks will try to select a target to Raise, if Retargeting of any Raise Feature is enabled.
You can find Raise Features under PvE>General, or under each caster that has a Raise.
If there are fewer than 5 items, and all return nothing when checked, will fall back to: your Hard Target if they're dead, or <Any Dead Party Member>.
These targets will only be considered valid if they are friendly, dead, and within 30y.

data.name: RetargetHealingActionsToStack_helpMark
data.value: Will Retarget all Single-Target Healing Actions to the Heal Stack as shown below, similarly to how Redirect or Reaction would. This ensures that the target used to check HP% threshold logic for healing actions is the same target that will receive that heal.

data.name: RetargetHealingActionsToStack_recommendedValue
data.value: On (If you customize the Heal Stack AT ALL)

data.name: PerformanceMode_helpMark
data.value: This mode will disable actions being changed on your hotbar, but will still continue to work in the background as you press your buttons.

data.name: DuplicateActionPresses_helpMark
data.value: Shows the button-press pulse animation on every hotbar copy of an action, not just the slot you actually pressed.

data.name: ShortDTRText_helpMark
data.value: Will hide the number of active Auto-Mode Combos. By default the Server Info Bar shows: - Whether Auto-Rotation is on or off - (if on) The number of active Auto-Mode Combos - (if applicable) Whether another plugin is controlling the state of Auto-Rotation.

data.name: ShowBorderAroundOptionsWithChildren_helpMark
data.value: Will draw a border around Combos and Features that have Features and Options of their own.

data.name: ShowBorderAroundOptionsWithChildren_Name
data.value: Show Borders around Combos and Features with Options

data.name: ShowOpenerDtr_helpMark
data.value: Shows the status of your current opener, if enabled and applicable.

data.name: ShowOpenerDtr_Name
data.value: Opener Status in Server Info Bar

data.name: ShowTargetHighlight_extraText
data.value: (Only used by AST and DNC currently)

data.name: ShowTargetHighlight_helpMark
data.value: Draws a box around party members in the vanilla Party List, when targeted by certain Features.

data.name: SuppressAutorotCommand_helpMark
data.value: Will hide chat feedback for /qoltweaks auto commands

data.name: SuppressSetCommands_helpMark
data.value: Will hide chat feedback for /qoltweaks set and /qoltweaks unset commands. (Will still show feedback if the command is being overriden by IPC, or fails)

data.name: SuppressSetCommands_Name
data.value: Suppress Set and Unset commands feedback

data.name: TargetHighlightColor_helpMark
data.value: Controls the color of the box drawn around party members.

data.name: Throttle_helpMark
data.value:
Will restrict how often Combos will update the Action on your Hotbar. At 50ms it's not really restrictive, always giving you an up to date action.
If you are looking for some (fairly minor) FPS gains then you can increase this value to make Combos run less often. This makes your combos less responsive, and perhaps even clips GCDs. At high values this will clip your GCDs by several seconds or break your rotation altogether.

data.name: UIShowPresetIDs_helpMark
data.value: Displays the Preset ID number next to the name of each Combo and Feature. These are the IDs used for commands like `/qoltweaks toggle <ID>`. Pre-7.3 the behavior was to show a number here, but it was much shorter, and did not work in commands.

data.name: UIShowPresetIDs_Name
data.value: Show Preset IDs next to Combo Names

data.name: UIShowSearchBar_helpMark
data.value: Controls whether Search Bars should be shown in Settings, and PvE and PvP Jobs.

data.name: UseCustomHealStack_helpMark
data.value:
Select this if you would rather make your own stack of target priorities for Heal Targets instead of using our default stack.
It is recommended to use this to align with your Redirect/Reaction configuration IF you're not using the Retarget Healing Actions option above; otherwise it is preference.

data.name: UseCustomHealStack_Name
data.value: Use a Custom Heal Stack Instead

data.name: UseFieldMouseoverOverridesInDefaultHealStack_helpMark
data.value:
Will add any MouseOver targets to the top of the Default Heal Stack, overriding the rest of the stack if you are mousing over any party member UI.
It is recommended to enable this if you are a keyboard+mouse user and enable Retarget Healing Actions (or have UI MouseOver targets in your Redirect/Reaction configuration).

data.name: UseFieldMouseoverOverridesInDefaultHealStack_Name
data.value: Add Field MouseOver to the Default Healing Stack

data.name: UseFocusTargetOverrideInDefaultHealStack_helpMark
data.value: This will add your focus target under your hard and soft targets in the Default Heal Stack, overriding the rest of the stack if you have a living focus target.

data.name: UseFocusTargetOverrideInDefaultHealStack_Name
data.value: Add Focus Target to the Default Healing Stack

data.name: UseLowestHPOverrideInDefaultHealStack_helpMark
data.value: This will add a nearby party member with the lowest HP% to bottom of the Default Heal Stack, overriding only yourself.

data.name: UseLowestHPOverrideInDefaultHealStack_Name
data.value: Add Lowest HP% Ally to the Default Healing Stack

data.name: UseLowestHPOverrideInDefaultHealStack_warningMark
data.value: Unlike the other Default+ Options, this one is not an option in most other Retargeting Plugins. THIS SHOULD BE USED WITH THE 'RETARGET HEALING ACTIONS' SETTING ABOVE!

data.name: UseUIMouseoverOverridesInDefaultHealStack_helpMark
data.value:
Will add any UI MouseOver targets to the top of the Default Heal Stack, overriding the rest of the stack if you are mousing over any party member UI.
It is recommended to enable this if you are a keyboard+mouse user and enable Retarget Healing Actions (or have UI MouseOver targets in your Redirect/Reaction configuration).

data.name: UseUIMouseoverOverridesInDefaultHealStack_Name
data.value: Add UI MouseOver to the Default Healing Stack

data.name: AprilFools2026_helpMark
data.value: For when you can't get enough MNK in your plugin.

data.name: AprilFools2026_recommendedValue
data.value: Off, unless your name starts with Beep

data.name: AprilFools2026_defaultValue
data.value: On, cause it's that type of day y'know

data.name: TankbusterTTS_helpMark
data.value: Will play a text-to-speech sound clip whenever a tankbuster is detected, including who it is targeting.

data.name: AoEDamageTTS_helpMark
data.value: Will play a text-to-speech sound clip whenever a stack/group damage cast is detected, including who it is targeting if applicable.

data.name: TankbusterToast_helpMark
data.value: Show a message on-screen whenever a tankbuster is detected, including who it is targeting.

data.name: AoEDamageToast_helpMark
data.value: Show a message on-screen whenever a stack/group damage cast is detected, including who it is targeting if applicable.
